title: Track a Satellite Pass
description: Nova helps you follow an overhead satellite using your telescope.

dialogue.text: A bright satellite is passing tonight. We'll predict its path, practice smooth tracking, and log the time like orbital analysts.

options.text: How do I find the path?
I'm already outside with the scope.

dialogue.text: Use a tracking app or online predictor to note the rise time, maximum elevation, and direction of travel. Pick a pass brighter than magnitude 2 for easier tracking.

options.text: Path and times are in my mission logbook
Looking for a higher pass instead.

dialogue.text: Level the tripod, keep the telescope capped until you're clear of the Sun, and aim ahead of the predicted path. Practice with a slow pan across a distant tree or chimney to warm up.

options.text: Running a practice sweep before the pass.
Kept the satellite in sight across the sky

dialogue.text: Use gentle, continuous motions instead of jerky jumps, and lead the path by a few degrees. If you lose it, stop and let it drift back into view rather than sweeping wildly.

options.text: Reset and try the next pass.
Recovered it and logged the time.

dialogue.text: Great tracking! Recording the time and path helps refine its orbit. Close the app, cap the optics, and stow the tripod before moving in the dark.
